title: Thelypteris sanctiformis
taxonomy: Thelypteris sanctiformis is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This family is a part of the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida are part of the division Pteridophyta, also known as the ferns. The division Pteridophyta belongs to the kingdom Plantae, which contains all plant species. The classification of Thelypteris sanctiformis reflects its evolutionary relationship to other ferns.
uses: Thelypteris sanctiformis is known to have various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its leaves are said to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal benefits. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.
